reasoning: The required threshold for resolution is around 17 'Riots' within a 30-day period (10 * 1.667). The historical average in Gabon over the last year is only 0.667 per 30-day period, rendering this threshold extraordinarily high. It would demand severe, sustained civil unrest (a riot every ~2 days). The only likely catalyst for such an event is a massively contested election. The presidential election was held on April 12, 2025. By April 13, it was clear that interim President Brice Oligui Nguema won a landslide victory with over 90% of the vote. Reports indicate the election day was largely calm, the electorate is 'relieved', and there is no evidence of significant protests or violence during the campaign or immediately post-election. Without any current escalation, the chances of hitting the required quota by the upcoming dates are extremely remote. While uncertainty naturally scales with time (hence slightly higher probabilities for July and October, mostly due to unforeseen catalysts), the threshold remains incredibly hard to breach under current political stability.
